surface_verification.justification: No application runtime surface: this changes a CI workflow-template shell gate (shared/templates/github-actions/security.yml.template) shipped by /shipwright-adopt, plus its pytest regression test. The boundary (raw scanner SARIF -> merge block/pass decision) is verified empirically by executing the SHIPPED gate shell (extracted from the template, no logic drift) against synthetic SARIF fixtures across 9 cases (RED on the old result-level filter -> GREEN on the fix) AND against the real WebUI scanner SARIF (semgrep 130 / trivy 12 max 7.5 / gitleaks 1): the corrected gate reports 0 false criticals and blocks on the 1 real secret. No web/UI/API/store/SSE surface.

behaviors.behavior: Gate blocks when a finding's rule-level security-severity >= 9.0 (resolved result.ruleId -> tool.driver.rules[].properties) -- the exact shape the old result-level read missed
behaviors.evidence: test_security_critical_gate.py::test_rule_level_critical_blocks PASSED (RED on old template, GREEN on fix)

behaviors.behavior: Gate resolves severity via ruleIndex fallback when a result references its rule only by index (no ruleId)
Gate resolves severity per-result (a 9.5 rule elsewhere does NOT block a result pointing at a sub-critical rule)
A non-numeric security-severity (future/3rd scanner) degrades to not-critical via try/catch instead of wedging the gate under set -e
Gate blocks on any result emitted by the Gitleaks run (a committed secret always blocks, regardless of severity score)
behaviors.evidence: test_security_critical_gate.py::test_secret_blocks PASSED + empirical real WebUI gitleaks.sarif -> blocks

behaviors.behavior: Gate does NOT block on scanner findings that carry no security-severity (e.g. Semgrep --config auto) -- a critical gate firing on 130 auto-findings would be unusable
Gate passes a clean scan (trivy < 9.0 + semgrep no-severity + gitleaks ran with no secret)
Gate fails CLOSED when sarif/ is empty (every scanner crashed / produced no output)
Gate fails CLOSED when a SARIF file is invalid JSON (a scanner emitted a broken report)
